articles.title: Fenix Resources Delivers Record Quarterly Production
articles.summary: Fenix Resources has reported record quarterly production of 1.1Mt at its Iron Ridge project, with cash costs falling to $65/wmt. The company remains on track to meet its FY26 guidance of 4.0-4.5Mt.

articles.title: Iron Ore Prices Rally on Chinese Stimulus Hopes
articles.summary: Iron ore futures rose above $108/t as China signalled additional infrastructure spending to support economic growth. The rally boosts Australian iron ore miners including BHP, Rio Tinto, and Fortescue.

articles.title: AUD Weakens as RBA Holds Rates Steady
articles.summary: The Australian dollar fell to $0.648 after the RBA held interest rates steady at 4.10%. The weaker currency provides a tailwind for ASX-listed commodity exporters.

articles.title: Fenix Resources Declares 2.0 Cents Fully Franked Dividend
articles.summary: Fenix Resources has declared an interim dividend of 2.0 cents per share fully franked, representing a payout ratio of approximately 50% of net profit. The stock currently yields 8.25%.

articles.title: China Steel Output Rises as Construction Season Begins
articles.summary: Chinese steel production rose 3.2% in March as the spring construction season got underway. Demand for iron ore is expected to remain robust through Q2.

articles.title: Iron Ore Supply Constraints Support Prices
articles.summary: Ongoing supply disruptions from Brazilian operations and declining grades at Australian mines are supporting iron ore prices above $100/t, benefiting low-cost producers.
